slides.title: The strongest part of a lift is the *way down*.
slides.sub: Slowing the lowering phase of any rep can build strength and resilient tendons. Here is why it works.

slides.title: Train the lowering phase
slides.body: Every rep has a lift and a lower. The slow lowering, the *eccentric phase*, is where much of the strength is built.

slides.title: Muscles are stronger lengthening
slides.body: Your muscles can handle more load while lengthening than while shortening. Slow lowering *puts that strength to work*.

slides.title: Stiffer, healthier tendons
slides.body: Controlled lowering is linked to greater *tendon stiffness*, which can support stronger joints and steadier movement as you age.

slides.title: More signal, less strain
slides.body: Eccentric work can trigger a strong *muscle building response* at a lower metabolic cost, so you grow without burning out.

slides.title: Count three on the way down
slides.body: Take three slow seconds to lower the weight on each rep. Stay controlled, then lift normally and repeat.

slides.title: Make every rep *count for more*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your training, recovery, sleep and steps, then turns it all into one daily Health Score so progress stays clear.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Most people rush the way down. That slow lower is where strength and tendon health are quietly built. 🌿

Lengthening under load builds strength, stiffens tendons and triggers growth at a lower cost. Just count three seconds down. Save this for your next session. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your training and recovery into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why the Slow Lowering Phase Builds Real Strength
